title: Rare Species and Adaptation
text:
A range of endangered species have returned to the exclusion zone. This includes Przewalski's horses, reintroduced in 1998 as a conservation experiment.

Both Eurasian lynx and European bison have returned and established their populations. Several bird species have also returned, including black storks, white storks and white-tailed eagles.

Most significant is the return of the globally endangered greater spotted eagle, which depends on wetland habitats and is very sensitive to human disturbance.

There is also scientific evidence that some species appear to be adapting to the radioactive environment. Tree frogs in the zone are darker, as higher melanin levels seem to protect against radiation damage.

Research on wolves indicates possible adaptations that may help them survive chronic radiation exposure and reduce cancer risks.

quizlet: находящийся под угрозой исчезновения во всем мире

questions.question: Which species was reintroduced in 1998 as a conservation experiment?
questions.explanation: The text states that Przewalski’s horses were reintroduced in 1998 as a conservation experiment.

questions.question: Which bird is described as globally endangered and sensitive to human disturbance?
questions.explanation: The passage describes the greater spotted eagle as globally endangered and very sensitive to human disturbance.

questions.question: Why are tree frogs in the zone darker?
options: They have higher melanin levels that may protect against radiation damage
They have less food than other frogs
They are covered with ash from the reactor
questions.explanation: The text says tree frogs are darker because higher melanin levels seem to protect against radiation damage.

questions.question: What does research on Eurasian wolves suggest?
options: They have completely disappeared from the zone
They may be developing adaptations to survive chronic radiation
questions.explanation: The passage says research on Eurasian wolves indicates potential adaptations to survive chronic radiation and reduce cancer risks.

questions.question: What is the main focus of Part C?
options: The return of rare species and possible adaptation to radiation
The economic value of nuclear power
The evacuation of people from Ukraine
questions.explanation: This section focuses on endangered species returning and some organisms adapting to the radioactive environment.
